title: 帷幕
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Hastings discuss five seemingly unrelated murder cases involving different motives, such as jealousy, money, and selflessness. Poirot points out that despite their differences, each case has only one clear suspect. He reveals that a mysterious individual, referred to as "X," was present near the victims or locations in all five instances, despite having no motive and being hundreds of miles away during the actual crimes. Hastings concludes that X is the real killer.

Poirot then informs Hastings that X is currently present in Styles Manor and predicts that a murder will soon occur in the house. When Hastings suggests Poirot can prevent the crime, Poirot explains the difficulty of prevention. He outlines three methods: warning the victim, which often fails due to disbelief; or using cunning to intervene at the exact moment of the strike to catch the murderer in the act. Poirot notes that they currently do not know who the target will be because the motive remains unknown. Hastings considers whether the motive is revenge, but Poirot refuses to reveal the identity of X to prevent Hastings from reacting visibly when they encounter the suspect.

The conversation is interrupted by the arrival of Hastings' daughter, Judith, who is described as tall with a serious, tragic air. Poirot teases her about her scientific work, noting her fingers are stained with methylene blue.
